main_paper.raw: {"methods":["Graph statistics-based structural similarity metric for MILP instance classification","Self-supervised learning GNN-based embedding similarity metric for MILP instance classification","Novel classification algorithm for categorizing MILP instances based on structure and embedding similarity","Comprehensive benchmarking of traditional exact solvers, heuristic algorithms, and ML-based approaches on the new dataset"],"problems":["Lack of standardized, well-categorized benchmark datasets for ML-based MILP research","Difficulty in comparing and evaluating ML-based MILP algorithms due to heterogeneous and unsorted existing datasets","Need for fine-grained classification of MILP instances to enable fair and meaningful benchmarking"],"datasets":["ML4MILP (proposed in this paper): 100,000 MILP instances across more than 70 heterogeneous classes"],"metrics":["Graph statistics-based structural similarity score","GNN-based embedding similarity score","Objective function value","Optimality gap"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["Introduction of ML4MILP, a new open-source benchmark dataset with 100,000 MILP instances across 70+ classes, specifically designed for ML-based optimization research.","Development of a novel classification algorithm using graph statistics and GNN-based embedding similarity to categorize MILP instances.","Provision of nearly optimal solutions for each instance using an adaptive constraint-partitioned algorithm.","Comprehensive benchmarking and ranking of mainstream MILP algorithms on the new dataset, revealing robustness issues in some previously claimed state-of-the-art methods.","ML4MILP is open-source, scalable, and intended for regular updates to support ongoing research."]}

methods: Graph statistics-based structural similarity metric for MILP instance classification
Self-supervised learning GNN-based embedding similarity metric for MILP instance classification
Novel classification algorithm for categorizing MILP instances based on structure and embedding similarity
Comprehensive benchmarking of traditional exact solvers, heuristic algorithms, and ML-based approaches on the new dataset
problems: Lack of standardized, well-categorized benchmark datasets for ML-based MILP research
Difficulty in comparing and evaluating ML-based MILP algorithms due to heterogeneous and unsorted existing datasets
Need for fine-grained classification of MILP instances to enable fair and meaningful benchmarking
datasets: ML4MILP (proposed in this paper): 100,000 MILP instances across more than 70 heterogeneous classes
novelty_claims: Introduction of ML4MILP, a new open-source benchmark dataset with 100,000 MILP instances across 70+ classes, specifically designed for ML-based optimization research.
Development of a novel classification algorithm using graph statistics and GNN-based embedding similarity to categorize MILP instances.
Provision of nearly optimal solutions for each instance using an adaptive constraint-partitioned algorithm.
Comprehensive benchmarking and ranking of mainstream MILP algorithms on the new dataset, revealing robustness issues in some previously claimed state-of-the-art methods.
ML4MILP is open-source, scalable, and intended for regular updates to support ongoing research.

methods: Distributional MIPLIB: a curated multi-domain library of MILP problem distributions with standardized test sets and hardness levels
ML-guided variable branching evaluation on new distributions
Learning branching policies from mixed distributions (multi-domain training)
problems: Lack of a standardized, multi-domain repository of MILP distributions for ML-guided methods
Difficulty in benchmarking and comparing ML-based MILP methods due to heterogeneous or synthetic datasets
Limited evaluation on real-world MILP domains and lack of accessible real-world datasets
Need for comprehensive evaluation of ML-guided MILP methods across domains and hardness levels
datasets: Distributional MIPLIB (introduced in this paper): curated from ten synthetic and real-world problems used in ML for MILPs and three new real-world problems
Synthetic domains: Set Covering (SC), Combinatorial Auction (CA), Maximum Independent Set (MIS), Capacitated Facility Location Problem (CFLP), etc.
Real-world domains: production packing, electric grid optimization, etc.
metrics: Performance of ML-guided variable branching (e.g., solution time, node count, generalization to larger instances)
Comparison of mixed vs. homogeneous distribution training (e.g., performance improvement, generalization)

results.metric: Performance of mixed distribution training vs. homogeneous
results.value: Mixed distributions achieve better performance compared to homogeneous distributions when data is limited and generalize well to larger instances (quantitative values not specified in provided text)

novelty_claims: First comprehensive, multi-domain library (Distributional MIPLIB) for ML-guided MILP research, with standardized test sets and hardness levels
Curation of both synthetic and real-world MILP distributions, including three real-world problems not previously used in ML for MILPs
Provision of pre-generated training, validation, and test sets, as well as instance generators for most problems
Demonstration that learning branching policies from mixed distributions improves performance and generalization compared to single-domain training
Enables benchmarking and comparison of ML-based MILP methods across diverse domains and problem scales

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Predict-and-search framework for MILP","Graph Neural Network (GNN) to predict marginal probability of each variable","Trust region-based search for feasible solutions around GNN predictions"],"problems":["Efficiently finding high-quality feasible solutions for mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) problems","Reducing sample collection cost for supervised learning in MILP solution prediction","Ensuring feasibility of solutions predicted by end-to-end ML approaches for MILP"],"datasets":["Several public benchmark MILP datasets (not named specifically in the provided text)"],"metrics":["Primal gap"],"results":[{"metric":"Primal gap improvement over SCIP","value":"51.1%"},{"metric":"Primal gap improvement over Gurobi","value":"9.9%"}],"novelty_claims":["Proposes a novel predict-and-search framework that combines GNN-based prediction with a trust region search for feasible MILP solutions.","Reduces the need for collecting abundant optimal solutions by approximating the solution distribution and weighing high-quality solutions by their objective values.","Addresses feasibility by searching within a trust region around the predicted solution, rather than fixing variables as in prior end-to-end approaches.","Demonstrates significant improvements in primal gap over state-of-the-art solvers SCIP and Gurobi."]}

methods: Predict-and-search framework for MILP
Graph Neural Network (GNN) to predict marginal probability of each variable
Trust region-based search for feasible solutions around GNN predictions
problems: Efficiently finding high-quality feasible solutions for mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) problems
Reducing sample collection cost for supervised learning in MILP solution prediction
Ensuring feasibility of solutions predicted by end-to-end ML approaches for MILP
datasets: Several public benchmark MILP datasets (not named specifically in the provided text)
novelty_claims: Proposes a novel predict-and-search framework that combines GNN-based prediction with a trust region search for feasible MILP solutions.
Reduces the need for collecting abundant optimal solutions by approximating the solution distribution and weighing high-quality solutions by their objective values.
Addresses feasibility by searching within a trust region around the predicted solution, rather than fixing variables as in prior end-to-end approaches.
Demonstrates significant improvements in primal gap over state-of-the-art solvers SCIP and Gurobi.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Learning-based Large Neighborhood Search (LNS) algorithm for MIPs","Neural Diving model for initial assignment generation","Neural Neighborhood Selection policy for neighborhood selection at each LNS step","Imitation learning to train the neighborhood selection policy using an expert policy"],"problems":["Efficiently solving large-scale Mixed Integer Programs (MIPs)","Improving the scalability and performance of LNS algorithms for combinatorial optimization","Learning effective neighborhood selection strategies for LNS in MIPs"],"datasets":["Five real-world MIP datasets (including two from production systems at Google)"],"metrics":["Average primal gap"],"results":[{"metric":"Average primal gap improvement","value":"2x to 37.8x better than the best baseline on three datasets at large running times"}],"novelty_claims":["First scalable learning-based LNS algorithm that combines learned models for both initial assignment and neighborhood selection in MIPs","Imitation learning approach using an expert policy guaranteed to select the optimal neighborhood under certain assumptions","Demonstrates scalability and effectiveness on large-scale real-world MIP datasets, outperforming all baselines","Ability to model the joint distribution of a large number of simultaneous decisions for both assignment and neighborhood selection, enabling high-dimensional generative models for scalable training and inference"]}

results.value: 2x to 37.8x better than the best baseline on three datasets at large running times

novelty_claims: First scalable learning-based LNS algorithm that combines learned models for both initial assignment and neighborhood selection in MIPs
Imitation learning approach using an expert policy guaranteed to select the optimal neighborhood under certain assumptions

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Graph Convolutional Neural Network (GCNN) for learning branch-and-bound variable selection policies","Imitation learning from strong branching expert rule using behavioral cloning with cross-entropy loss","Encoding MILP variable-constraint bipartite graphs as input to GCNN"],"problems":["Learning effective variable selection (branching) policies for branch-and-bound in mixed-integer linear programs (MILPs)","Improving generalization and performance of machine learning-based branching policies on combinatorial optimization problems"],"datasets":["Set covering problem instances","Combinatorial auction problem instances","Capacitated facility location problem instances","Maximum independent set problem instances"],"metrics":["Solving time","Optimality gap","Generalization to larger instances"],"results":[{"metric":"Solving time","value":"Substantial improvement over traditional branching rules and previous machine learning methods"},{"metric":"Generalization","value":"Policies generalize well to instances significantly larger than those seen during training"},{"metric":"Comparison to expert rules","value":"First time improvement over expert-designed branching rules in a state-of-the-art solver on large problems"}],"novelty_claims":["First to encode branching policies for MILPs using a graph convolutional neural network leveraging the natural bipartite graph structure, reducing manual feature engineering.","First to improve over expert-designed branching rules implemented in a state-of-the-art solver on large problems.","Use of behavioral cloning with cross-entropy loss to approximate strong branching decisions, which is less difficult than previous approaches predicting scores or rankings."]}

methods: Graph Convolutional Neural Network (GCNN) for learning branch-and-bound variable selection policies
Imitation learning from strong branching expert rule using behavioral cloning with cross-entropy loss
Encoding MILP variable-constraint bipartite graphs as input to GCNN
problems: Learning effective variable selection (branching) policies for branch-and-bound in mixed-integer linear programs (MILPs)
Improving generalization and performance of machine learning-based branching policies on combinatorial optimization problems

results.value: Substantial improvement over traditional branching rules and previous machine learning methods
Policies generalize well to instances significantly larger than those seen during training
First time improvement over expert-designed branching rules in a state-of-the-art solver on large problems

novelty_claims: First to encode branching policies for MILPs using a graph convolutional neural network leveraging the natural bipartite graph structure, reducing manual feature engineering.
First to improve over expert-designed branching rules implemented in a state-of-the-art solver on large problems.
Use of behavioral cloning with cross-entropy loss to approximate strong branching decisions, which is less difficult than previous approaches predicting scores or rankings.

selected_papers.raw: {"methods":["Replacing key heuristic components of combinatorial optimization solvers with machine learning models","Designing application-specific algorithms using historical data","Using machine learning to enhance exact solvers for combinatorial optimization","Providing a unified OpenAI Gym-like API for solver control tasks via Ecole"],"problems":["Improving combinatorial optimization solvers using machine learning","Finding the best feasible solution for combinatorial optimization problems","Producing the tightest optimality certificate (best branching decisions)","Finding the best solver configuration (parameter tuning) for combinatorial optimization problems","Adapting solvers to specific problem distributions using historical data"],"datasets":["Balanced item placement","Workload apportionment","Maritime inventory routing (kept anonymous for contestants)"],"metrics":["Quality of feasible solution (primal task)","Tightness of optimality certificate (dual task)","Solver configuration performance (configuration task)"],"results":[],"novelty_claims":["First large-scale competition to systematically evaluate machine learning methods for improving combinatorial optimization solvers on realistic, application-specific benchmarks.","Introduces three distinct control tasks (primal, dual, configuration) for machine learning in combinatorial optimization, each mapped to real-world scenarios.","Provides a unified API (via Ecole) for interacting with the open-source solver SCIP, enabling reproducible and standardized evaluation of ML-based solver enhancements.","Focuses on the practical scenario of repeatedly solving instances from a specific distribution, leveraging historical data to design better solvers."]}

methods: Replacing key heuristic components of combinatorial optimization solvers with machine learning models

datasets: Maritime inventory routing (kept anonymous for contestants)
novelty_claims: First large-scale competition to systematically evaluate machine learning methods for improving combinatorial optimization solvers on realistic, application-specific benchmarks.
Introduces three distinct control tasks (primal, dual, configuration) for machine learning in combinatorial optimization, each mapped to real-world scenarios.
Provides a unified API (via Ecole) for interacting with the open-source solver SCIP, enabling reproducible and standardized evaluation of ML-based solver enhancements.
Focuses on the practical scenario of repeatedly solving instances from a specific distribution, leveraging historical data to design better solvers.

results.value: Better solutions found with 1/2 or 1/5 of SCIP's runtime

Advantages persist when policies are applied to much larger problems
Outperforms Gurobi within the same time limit

novelty_claims: Proposes a high-level, learning-based LNS method for general integer programming problems using deep RL.
Factorizes the combinatorial action space of variable subset selection into binary decisions, enabling tractable RL policy learning.
Designs a GNN-based policy network to learn policies for each variable in parallel.
Trains the policy with a customized actor-critic algorithm.
Removes the need for a fixed cardinality of destroyed variable subsets, allowing adaptive and instance-dependent LNS steps.
Demonstrates generalization to larger problem instances and across different solvers (SCIP, Gurobi).

novelty_claims: Introduction of YAHPO Gym, a surrogate-based benchmark library with over 700 multi-fidelity, multi-objective HPO problems across 14 scenarios

Demonstration that tabular benchmarks can induce bias in HPO method ranking, while surrogate benchmarks like YAHPO Gym provide more faithful results
YAHPO Gym enables fast, cheap, and high-quality evaluation of HPO methods, suitable for large-scale meta-learning and meta-optimization studies
Provision of versioned surrogate models and extensive documentation for community use and extension

novelty_claims: Proposes a novel LNS framework (NLNS) that integrates learned heuristics via a deep neural network for the repair operator, specifically designed for LNS search settings.
Introduces a new neural network model with attention mechanism for completing incomplete solutions in VRP.
NLNS is adapted to leverage GPU parallelism for efficiency.
The approach is general and does not require problem-specific components, learning heuristics for a class of problem instances.
